note: Archive of previously proposed and considered strategist candidates. Prevents re-proposal. Append-only; entries are never removed, only superseded.

archived_candidates.title: Loop Health Monitor
archived_candidates.rationale: Detect and surface when the autonomy loop is down. Customer demand from issue #66.

archived_candidates.title: Model-Proxy Resource Guarantee
archived_candidates.rationale: Stop silent run-slot leaks. Customer demand from issue #67.

archived_candidates.title: Cost Accountability Per Loop Gate
archived_candidates.rationale: Aggregate cost visibility needed for portability and operator trust. Competitor gap from SWE-agent cost pain.

archived_candidates.title: Multi-Provider Model Routing For Loop Roles
archived_candidates.rationale: Customer demand: single-provider brittleness (403 failures in issue #67, PM budget unavailability). Competitor gap: OpenHands multi-backend ACP. Improves robustness and portability.
archived_candidates.falsified_by: Routing doesn't improve cost/quality tradeoffs, or model-specific prompt engineering makes routing infeasible.

archived_candidates.title: Output Quality Benchmark Gate
archived_candidates.rationale: Analogous field: DGM validates every code change against SWE-bench. Fills proof merit criterion gap: OA tracks process but not output quality.
archived_candidates.falsified_by: Standard coding benchmarks don't distinguish OA-produced code quality, or benchmark cost exceeds benefit.

archived_candidates.title: Substrate Complexity Reduction For Adopter Portability
archived_candidates.rationale: Competitor gap: SWE-agent migration to mini-SWE-agent (100-line Python). Analogous field: DGM issue #31 unreachable CLI modes. Portability merit criterion blocked by surface area.
archived_candidates.falsified_by: Usability surveys show adopters don't find the substrate complex, or simplification doesn't reduce onboarding time.

archived_candidates.title: Agent-Level Evidence Gates For Reviewer Verifiability
archived_candidates.rationale: Competitor gap: OpenHands issue #14857 explores reviewer-facing evidence gates per agent action. OA's proof_gate model stops at roadmap-item level; reviewer has no structured evidence per action to independently verify it was justified. Serves proof merit criterion at finer granularity.
archived_candidates.falsified_by: Reviewers do not actually inspect per-action evidence artifacts in practice, OR the cost of emitting structured evidence per action exceeds the verification benefit.

archived_candidates.title: Standardized Agent Trajectory Export For Bench And Replay
archived_candidates.rationale: Competitor gap: SWE-agent #1310 adds explicit trajectory hooks for remote environments. OA decision records are workflow-specific JSON with no standardized replay schema. A standard trajectory format unlocks bench, replay, quality scoring, and pattern mining — directly serving bench-autonomy-ratio and quality-benchmark-gate.
archived_candidates.falsified_by: Existing decision records already support replay and cross-run analysis without standardization; trajectory schema effort does not unlock new capability.

archived_candidates.title: Self-Improving Prompt Evolution For Loop Roles
archived_candidates.rationale: Analogous field: DGM's core innovation (evolving agent prompts through mutation/selection), plus Google AlphaEvolve (evolutionary search over agent capabilities). OA hand-crafts every prompt per role; automated prompt evolution treats prompts as evolvable artifacts improving autonomously against bench scores. Serves the constitution's method of absorbing best ideas from the field and feeds quality-benchmark-gate.
archived_candidates.falsified_by: Automated prompt mutation does not produce reliably better prompts than hand-crafting, OR bench variance is too high to distinguish improved variants from noise.

archived_candidates.title: Agent Episodic Memory For Cross-Issue Context Retrieval
archived_candidates.rationale: Competitor gap: SWE-agent #1440 proposes episodic memory via Dakera for recalling successful patch patterns across issues. OA's durable-decision-memory captures records but doesn't make them retrievable as context for future runs. Each agent re-derives solutions to similar problems. Serves absorb-best-ideas method and low-human-toil criterion.
archived_candidates.falsified_by: Past solutions do not generalize to similar-but-not-identical issues, making retrieval noise exceed benefit, OR benchmark variance masks any improvement from extra context.

archived_candidates.title: Installation Supply Chain Audit For Self-Hosted Repos
archived_candidates.rationale: Competitor gap: SWE-agent #1429 dependency hardening signals supply chain risk awareness in autonomous engineering repos. OA's self-hosted-fleet expands installation surface without supply chain audit. Serves robustness merit criterion and adopter trust for portability.
archived_candidates.falsified_by: Standard GitHub Dependabot/npm audit already covers OA's dependency risk, making a dedicated audit item redundant, OR installations do not actually inherit dependency risk at the level that would justify automated scanning.

archived_candidates.title: Strategist Operational Data Feed From Bench And Run History
archived_candidates.rationale: Internal gap: strategist-roadmap-research scans only external sources; OA's own bench and run-ledger data is never read. bench-autonomy-ratio and quality-benchmark-gate generate operational metrics that should inform strategic decisions. Serves proof merit criterion (data-driven strategy) and closes the external-only data gap.
archived_candidates.falsified_by: Internal operational metrics do not change or improve strategist proposals compared to external-only research, making the data feed overhead not worth the integration cost.
